---
title: "Domcke lab Electronic Lab Notebook"
subtitle: "Integrate Git & Github and AI agents to your science"
author:
 - name: "Joey Choi"
   affiliation: University of Zurich
   email: wonhyung.choi@uzh.ch
date: last-modified
format:
    revealjs:
        embed-resources: true
        incremental: false
        slide-number: true
        transition: none
        theme: [simple, custom.scss]
        scrollable: true
        logo: image/image.png
---

# ELN and GitHub

---

## Let's think about....

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 [2 3 4 5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::


::: callout-note
## What should we value an ELN?
:::

- Easy-to-archive

- Easy read and Easy write

- Flexibility --- We all have different way to handle our missions!

. . .

::: callout-note
## What should we pursue as a researcher?
:::

- Reproducibility -- ex. Documentation

- Collaborative -- ex. Data share to other researcher

- Robust workflow

. . .

::: callout-caution
## Caution

We are vulnerable, fragile but busy
:::

---

## What we have been using

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 [2 3 4 5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

**1. Google Docs, Dropbox and OpenBis**

**(a.k.a. Cloud-based tools)**

::: callout-tip
## **Good**

- Easy to share
- Intuitive (User friendly)
- Globally used
:::

::: callout-warning
## **Lack of**

- Structured way to track change (version control)
:::

\

. . .

**2. Slack, Microsoft Teams**

**(a.k.a. Messaging tools)**

::: callout-tip
## Good

- Facilitate informal discussion
- Easy for file exchange
:::

::: callout-warning
## Lack of

- poor organized format
- unreproducible
:::

---

## Fragmented research tool {transition="convex"}

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 [2 3 4 5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

![](image/image%20copy%202.png){fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size: 0.4em; text-align: center; color: gray;"}
Chen, K. Y., Toro-Moreno, M., & Subramaniam, A. R. (2024). GitHub is an effective platform for collaborative and reproducible laboratory research. arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09344
:::

## Here is Git and GitHub!

### This is All-in-one package

- version tracking

- Universal file formats

- Collaboration

- etc...

::: callout-warning
## However

We need a bit of time to learn....
:::

## Here is Git and GitHub!
### This is All-in-one package
![](image/image copy 3.png){fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size: 0.4em; text-align: center; color: gray;"}
Chen, K. Y., Toro-Moreno, M., & Subramaniam, A. R. (2024). GitHub is an effective platform for collaborative and reproducible laboratory research. arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.09344
:::

---

# What? And How? ![](https://api.iconify.design/mdi:github.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}![](https://api.iconify.design/devicon:git.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}

---

## What's Git?

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 2 [3 4 5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

::: {.hl-purple}
![](https://api.iconify.design/devicon:git.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"} Git is a record system within your folder
:::

: Once you have a change **wherever** in your file, git will record.

\

::: {.fragment}

### What's GitHub?

::: {.hl-blue}
![](https://api.iconify.design/mdi:github.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"} GitHub is a web-based code cloud
:::

: Once you have done your job, you can **upload** your code/files to GitHub, so others can access it.

\

::: {.callout-note}
## [Storage limit]{style="color:#cc0000"}
- 100 MB per file
- ~ 2GB per repository (ideally)
- Unlimited repo (No strict rules…) 
:::

:::

---

## How? 

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 2 [3 4 5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

### pipeline 


::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="50%"}
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
flowchart TD
   GitHub -->|pull| Local
    Local -->|push| GitHub
    Local --> Bench
    Bench -->|transcribe| Local

    style Bench fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f57f17
    style Local fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style GitHub fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
```

#### Personal project {style="text-align: center;"}
::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
```{mermaid}
%%| fig-align: center
flowchart TD
    GitHub
    GitHub -->|pull| Local1[Local 1]
    Local1 -->|push| GitHub
    GitHub -->|pull| Local2[Local 2]
    Local2 -->|push| GitHub
    Local1 <--> Bench1[bench 1]
    Local2 <--> Bench2[bench 2]

    style GitHub fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#01579b
    style Local1 fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style Local2 fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style Bench1 fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f57f17
    style Bench2 fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f57f17
```

#### Shared project {style="text-align: center;"}
::::

:::

\

### Folder structure

```text
project
├── ATAC_qPCR
│   ├── 20260706_primer_test
│   │   ├── note.md
│   │   ├── image.png
│   │   ├── table.csv
│   │   └── ...
│   └── 20260626_gDNA_titration
├── RoCK_ATAC
├── RoCK_ROI_bead
└── 10x_vs_BD
```

:::{.callout-important}
### The file structure can be varied as user defined
:::

---

# Markdown ![](https://api.iconify.design/mdi:language-markdown.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}

---

## All about Markdown ![](https://api.iconify.design/mdi:language-markdown.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 2 3 4 [5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

### What's Markdown?
Markdown is a lightweight markup language that you can use to add **formatting elements** to **plaintext text documents.** 

\

### Why Markdown?
> Markdown can be used for everything.\
> Markdown is portable.\
> Markdown is platform independent.\
>[from markdown guide](https://www.markdownguide.org/getting-started/)

---

## With markdown, we can make

::: {.absolute top="0" right="5%"}
::: {.sectionhead}
1 2 3 4 [5 6]{style="opacity:0.25"}
:::
:::

::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width="50%"}
Report
![](https://jozefhajnala.gitlab.io/r/img/r909-02-spinned.png)
::::

:::: {.column width="50%" .fragment}
TODO
![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ken-okabe/vscode-markdown-note-images/main/todo.gif)

:::: 

:::

....The usage of Markdown is [limitless]{.hl-yellow}

---

# Showcase

---

## Check list

<br/>

- [ ] Image drag and drop
- [ ] Adding codes
- [ ] Table insert
- [ ] Link insert
- [ ] Visual editor 
- [ ] Export the report to HTML/PDF/Word

<br/>

For more details about markdown![](https://api.iconify.design/mdi:language-markdown.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}, please click [here](https://www.markdownguide.org/extended-syntax/) 


# Data management with Claude Code ![](https://api.iconify.design/selfhst:claude.svg){height="1.2em" style="vertical-align: middle; display: inline;"}

---

## AI Agent [(feat.Claude Code)]{style="font-size: 0.5em"}

<br/>
Since AI agent appeared, AI is no longer limited to just [**chatbot**]{style="color:red"}. <br/>
AI is now actually able to do [**action**]{style="color:red"} in my local setup. <br/>

![](https://assets.xpnsec.com/an-evening-with-claude-code/cover.webp){fig-align="center"}

---

## Leveraging Claude code for our research

<br/>

::: {.incremental}

1. Read experimental logs 

2. Find out information of interest

3. Simple code/plot generation 

4. Manage GitHub repo  ...etc  

:::

::: {.fragment}
![](https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcRUEyC1D1OOC0z8wsHqMhzpvA_GViVq67-94fpuk9AeYA&s=10){fig-align="center"}
:::

---

## Does Claude do all of our jobs?

<br/>

NO. Claude [IS NOT ABLE]{style="color:red"} to replace 100% of your job


::: {.callout-caution}
## [CAUTION]{style="color:red"}
[Claude can even make your work worse]{style="color:red"}
:::

<br/>


> "Vibe coding" is great for prototypes.
> But dangerous production systems, security, and long-term code quality are on the line.
>  <br/> -Boris cherrny-
